title: quercus × nipponica
intro: Quercus × nipponica, also known as the Japanese white oak, is a hybrid species of deciduous tree that is native to Japan. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help to protect against cardiovascular disease and certain types of cancer. The health benefits of Quercus × nipponica include its potential to reduce blood pressure, improve immune function, and act as an anti-cancer agent. Herbal preparations of Quercus × nipponica include tea, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to support overall health and well-being.
